cases.prompt: 이 주제로 마인드맵 그려줘서 .canvas 파일로 만들어줘

assertions.text: It creates a .canvas file with the base structure containing nodes and edges arrays following the JSON Canvas spec.
It generates unique 16-character hexadecimal IDs for each node.
It gives each node the required fields id, type, x, y, width, and height.
It validates that all edge fromNode and toNode values reference existing node IDs and that JSON is parseable.

cases.prompt: create a canvas flowchart and connect the two boxes with a labeled arrow

assertions.text: It adds an edge with a unique id whose fromNode and toNode reference the two existing node IDs.
It sets a label on the edge and uses an arrow end so the connection reads as a directed flow.
It confirms both endpoints resolve to existing nodes after adding the connection.

cases.prompt: edit my .canvas file - move the project group node and recolor it green

assertions.text: It reads and parses the existing .canvas file, locates the target node by id, then modifies its position and color.
It uses a valid canvasColor value such as the green preset or a hex string.
It re-checks ID uniqueness and edge reference integrity after the edit.
